explanation: The natural language statement specifies a temporal relationship: after a certain event occurs (all self-test steps completed successfully), another state must eventually be reachable (proceed to Standby Mode). The atomic proposition 'selfTestPass' directly represents 'all self-test steps have been completed successfully'. The atomic proposition 'StandbyMode' represents 'Standby Mode'. The LTL formula G(selfTestPass -> F StandbyMode) translates this accurately: G (Globally) ensures that at any point in time, if 'selfTestPass' becomes true, then F (Eventually) 'StandbyMode' must become true. This precisely captures the 'once X, then it shall be possible to proceed to Y' requirement.

The property states that globally, if all self-test steps have been completed successfully (selfTestPass), then it shall be possible to proceed to the Standby Mode (F StandbyMode). This captures the requirement that the successful completion of self-tests enables the system to eventually transition to Standby Mode. The 'Once A, it shall be possible to proceed to B' structure is naturally translated as a global implication (G) from A to eventually B (F B).

The natural language statement "Once all self-test steps have been completed successfully, it shall be possible to proceed to the Standby Mode" implies a conditional guarantee. If the condition (all self-test steps completed successfully) is met, then a specific state (Standby Mode) must eventually be reachable or entered. The LTL formula captures this by stating that 'globally' (G), if 'selfTestPass' (all self-test steps passed successfully) occurs, then 'eventually' (F) 'StandbyMode' will be reached. This ensures that every time the self-test successfully completes, the system eventually transitions to the Standby Mode.

The natural language statement specifies a conditional temporal relationship: 'Once' a certain condition (all self-test steps completed successfully) is met, a future state (proceeding to Standby Mode) 'shall be possible'. The LTL formula captures this as a global implication. 'G' (Globally) ensures that this rule applies at all times. 'selfTestPass' represents the condition that all self-test steps have passed successfully. '->' (implies) means that if 'selfTestPass' becomes true, then what follows must eventually occur. 'F' (Eventually) means that 'StandbyMode' must become true at some point in the future. Therefore, the formula `G (selfTestPass -> F StandbyMode)` means 'Globally, if the self-test passes, then eventually it shall be possible to proceed to Standby Mode'.

The natural language statement "Once all self-test steps have been completed successfully, it shall be possible to proceed to the Standby Mode" implies a cause-and-effect relationship. The phrase "Once all self-test steps have been completed successfully" translates to the atomic proposition 'selfTestPass'. The phrase "it shall be possible to proceed to the Standby Mode" means that the system must eventually reach the 'StandbyMode'. Therefore, the LTL property states that globally, whenever 'selfTestPass' is true, it must eventually lead to 'StandbyMode' being true. This is captured by the 'G (P -> F Q)' pattern, where P is 'selfTestPass' and Q is 'StandbyMode', ensuring that the possibility to proceed is a guaranteed outcome after a successful self-test.

The property states that globally, if all self-test steps have passed successfully (`selfTestPass`), then it must eventually be possible to proceed to the Standby Mode (`F StandbyMode`). The 'Once X, it shall be possible to Y' structure is captured by the 'G (X -> F Y)' pattern, ensuring that the condition for entering Standby Mode is met and the system will eventually transition to that mode after the self-test completion.

The LTL property G(selfTestPass -> F StandbyMode) translates the requirement "Once all self-test steps have been completed successfully, it shall be possible to proceed to the Standby Mode." The G (Globally) operator ensures that this condition always holds. The implication (->) means that whenever 'selfTestPass' (all self-test steps completed successfully) occurs, then 'StandbyMode' must eventually (F) be reached or become possible. This accurately captures the temporal relationship where the successful completion of self-tests enables the possibility of entering Standby Mode.

The LTL formula G(selfTestPass -> F StandbyMode) captures the requirement that whenever all self-test steps have been completed successfully (represented by 'selfTestPass'), it shall eventually be possible to proceed to the Standby Mode (represented by 'F StandbyMode'). The 'G' (globally) operator ensures that this condition holds true at all times: every instance of 'selfTestPass' must be followed by an eventual 'StandbyMode'. This accurately reflects the 'once... then it shall be possible to proceed' temporal relationship.

The natural language statement specifies a conditional temporal relationship: 'Once all self-test steps have been completed successfully, it shall be possible to proceed to the Standby Mode.' This means that after the event of all self-test steps passing successfully (`selfTestPass`), the system must eventually enter Standby Mode (`StandbyMode`). The global operator 'G' ensures that this condition holds true at all times. The implication '->' establishes the dependency: if `selfTestPass` occurs, then `F StandbyMode` (eventually enter Standby Mode) must follow. This accurately captures the required behavior that successful self-test completion enables and leads to the Standby Mode.

The natural language statement implies a global temporal relationship: whenever all self-test steps are completed successfully, it must eventually be possible to proceed to Standby Mode. The 'once' suggests that this condition holds throughout the system's operation. Thus, if 'selfTestPass' occurs, then at some future point, 'StandbyMode' must be reachable. This is captured by the LTL formula G(selfTestPass -> F StandbyMode), which means 'Globally, if selfTestPass occurs, then eventually StandbyMode must occur'.
